posts.name: David Bueker
posts.cooked:
1990 Kalin Cellars Pinot Noir Cuvee DD
Served as part of a blind tasting, this had a cork that self-destructed, but after decanting off the bits it was a really interesting wine. I don’t know Kalin well enough to be sure if it was an intentional feature of the wine, or an artifact of a poor cork, but there was a definite decrepit, old wine cabbage aroma at first. Thankfully it largely blew off, though the aromas were always “old.” Where the fireworks went off was on the palate. In complete contrast to the aromas there was a bright, fresh floral note that took center stage. It was backed up by dusty red cherry fruit. Earth, mushroom, dried herb, stone dust, and all the other developed elements you could possibly have looked for were all there. The finish was long and fully resolved. It was fascinating to drink. I was convinced it was a much older Burgundy, and quite surprised at the reveal later on.
I loved the Kalin chardonnays I have tried, I should order some of the Pinot too. And more chard. I like how they hold the wine before release.
I have some of the '93 LD an as you say it is quite a unique and interesting wine, unlike any other Chardonnay I have ever drank.

posts.name: Barry Sunderland
posts.cooked: There’s a '79 Kalin PN on WineBid now…

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
Barry Sunderland:

There’s a '79 Kalin PN on WineBid now…

The one to kill for is the 1994 Cuvée Billon, two barrels made to commemorate the life of Gaston Billon, one of the Leightons’ favorite coopers. It’s the best US Pinot Noir I’ve ever tasted.

But there are a lot of good wines to look for. The 1997 DD is my favorite of the recent releases. We’re on the 1999 at the moment.

posts.name: Howard Horwitz
posts.cooked:
I love the 1990 Cuvée DD.

Any thoughts/opinions on their burgundies?

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
Howard Horwitz:

I love the 1990 Cuvée DD.

Any thoughts/opinions on their burgundies?

Terrific! They have 1er Cru Chouacheux and the lieu-dit Pierres Blanches which is on the slope above Grèves and commonly planted to white, but the same geology and altitude pretty much as Corton Clos du Roi.

But not much is made, so not easy to find, and 2001 is the latest release. In 2000 the PB is deeper and more complete, as Chouacheux was hailed, but in 2001 the Chouacheux is somewhat richer and more gourmand than the more ethereal PB, so the difference is more style than quality.
